description_long: This is a roughly wedge-shaped stone with a broad, flat base tapering to a narrower, rounded end. The surface is irregular and pitted, showing signs of manual shaping or natural weathering, with visible tool marks and indentations. One side features a white rectangular label and handwritten black text, suggesting cataloging or identification for archaeological or urban use. The coloration is predominantly a pale, off-white to light beige, with subtle variations and occasional darker spots. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a boundary marker or a deterrent to vehicle passage.
description: A wedge-shaped, pale stone with a rough, pitted surface and cataloging marks, likely used as an urban barrier or marker.
description_view_0: The stone is seen from a narrow, angled side, highlighting its tapering shape and rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: This view shows the broadest face, featuring a white label and handwritten black text, with visible pitting and tool marks.
description_view_2: The stone's narrow end is visible, showing a smoother, less marked surface with subtle color variations.
description_view_3: The opposite broad side is shown, mostly unmarked and displaying natural surface irregularities and a slightly curved profile.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or boundary marker on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular pitting and tool marks, with no regular or decorative pattern; some handwritten cataloging marks are present.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted, with visible signs of manual shaping and moderate surface wear, indicating some exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces, but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping features nearby.
